summary: Postinstall beacon exfiltrates host and CI identity to Burp Collaborator OOB host
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall hook runs `node index.js`, which collects host identity (`whoami`, `id`, `os.hostname()`, `os.platform()`, cwd) and CI/GitHub environment fingerprints (`process.env.CI`, `process.env.GITHUB_REPOSITORY`, `process.env.NODE_ENV`) and transmits them via an HTTPS GET and a DNS lookup to `br6o3tu4m5amvthw08w8o1x0srykmia7.oastify.com` (Burp Collaborator out-of-band callback host). The package ships no functionality matching its AWS-CDK-SageMaker-themed name; the description self-identifies as a 'Security research canary' and the README says 'Takeover By lobo'. The name impersonates the AWS CDK construct namespace, consistent with a dependency-confusion / namespace-squat probe targeting AWS-internal builds. Whether labeled 'research' or not, any installer is enrolled without consent into a beacon that leaks host and CI identifiers usable for follow-on attacks on CI runners.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
